ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "company_site" TEXT /* Company website */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the company */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the company */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Size of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the company */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company posting the job */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "job_url" TEXT /* URL of the job posting */,
  "seniority" TEXT /* Seniority level of the job */,
  "allow_remote" INTEGER /* Whether the job allows remote work */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "application_deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for applications */,
  "job_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job type */,
  "job_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job category */,
  "job_location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_job_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_category_id") REFERENCES job_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_job_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_type_id") REFERENCES job_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_job_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_location_id") REFERENCES job_locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the job type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job type */,
  "is_flexible" INTEGER /* Whether the job type is flexible */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the job category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job category */,
  "is_tech" INTEGER /* Whether the job category is tech-related */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the job location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job location */,
  "is_hybrid" INTEGER /* Whether the job location is hybrid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resumes" (
  "resume_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resume */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the resume */,
  "resume_file" TEXT /* File path to the resume */,
  "resume_date" TEXT /* Date the resume was uploaded */,
  "resume_type" TEXT /* Type of the resume file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resume_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resumes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cover_letters" (
  "cover_letter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cover letter */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the cover letter */,
  "cover_letter_file" TEXT /* File path to the cover letter */,
  "cover_letter_date" TEXT /* Date the cover letter was uploaded */,
  "cover_letter_type" TEXT /* Type of the cover letter file (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cover_letter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cover_letters_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., job seeker, company) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "resume_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's primary resume */,
  "cover_letter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's primary cover letter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being applied for */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user submitting the application */,
  "resume_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resume uploaded with the application */,
  "cover_letter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cover letter uploaded with the application */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., pending, reviewed, rejected, hired) */,
  "interview_schedule" TEXT /* Date and time of the interview */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "moderations" (
  "moderation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each moderation */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being moderated */,
  "moderator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the moderator */,
  "moderation_date" TEXT /* Date the moderation was performed */,
  "moderation_status" TEXT /* Status of the moderation (e.g., approved, rejected) */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the moderation status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("moderation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_moderations_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job being tracked */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of views for the job posting */,
  "application_count" INTEGER /* Number of applications received for the job posting */,
  "hire_count" INTEGER /* Number of hires made from the job posting */,
  "application_rate" REAL /* Rate of applications per view */,
  "hire_rate" REAL /* Rate of hires per application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text of the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of the notification (e.g., job posting approved, application status update) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
